steps.title: Identify the subject-verb boundary
steps.content: The subject is “the Huntington Library in California” (with “in California” modifying it), and the main verb is “received.” The blank occurs directly between them.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
No punctuation should separate a subject from its verb. Choice D is correct because it places no punctuation after “California,” allowing the subject “the Huntington Library in California” to flow directly into “received.” Choices A, B, and C all insert punctuation that breaks this rule.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
